subject: UX & Research
title: When Speed Kills
description: A quick, practical guide to When Speed Kills.

hook.text: Google found that adding 0.5 seconds to search results page load dropped traffic by 20%. Half a second.

buildup.text: Amazon reported that every 100 ms of added latency cost them 1% in sales. Speed is money.

discovery.text: Under 100ms feels instant. Between 100ms and 1 second, users notice but wait. Above 10 seconds, they leave.

twist.text: Sometimes the fix isn't faster servers. Progress bars and skeleton screens mask real load time.

climax.text: Performance is a UX feature, not just engineering. Users judge your product by how fast it feels, not how fast it is.

punchline.text: Speed is a feature. Half a second changes everything.

quiz.question: What did Google find about adding 0.5 seconds to search load time?
